mnemonics.title: OOP CONCEPTS
mnemonics.content: CUPID FOR INHERITANCE - CLASS (Blueprint) USES POLYMORPHISM TO INHERIT DIFFERENT FORMS. ENCAPSULATION HIDES DATA, ABSTRACTION SHOWS ESSENTIALS, INHERITANCE REUSES CODE.

mnemonics.title: PARADIGM TYPES
mnemonics.content: I SEE D O F L - IMPERATIVE, STRUCTURED, DECLARATIVE, OBJECT-ORIENTED, FUNCTIONAL, LOGIC. THE MAIN ONES TO REMEMBER.

mnemonics.title: PROCEDURAL VS OBJECT-ORIENTED
mnemonics.content: PROCEDURAL = FUNCTIONS + DATA SEPARATED. OBJECT-ORIENTED = FUNCTIONS AND DATA TOGETHER (ENCAPSULATED).

acronyms.usage: Programming paradigm that organizes code around objects containing data and methods
Four fundamental concepts of Object-Oriented Programming

acronyms.expansion: C is a general-purpose, procedural, middle-level programming language
acronyms.usage: The language being studied in this course, combining high-level and low-level features
Declarative language used for managing relational databases

visualTips.visualization: Think of Imperative as giving step-by-step directions to a stranger (TURN LEFT, THEN RIGHT), while Declarative as giving a destination address (GO TO MAIN STREET).
Imagine a CAPSULE containing medicine inside - just like a class contains data and methods together, hidden from outside access.
Think of a family tree - child inherits properties (eye color, hair color) from parents. Similarly, child class inherits properties from parent class.
Think of water - it can be ice, liquid, or vapor (many forms) but is still H2O. Objects can take many forms in OOP.
Think of a car steering wheel - you use it to turn (abstracted) without knowing the complex internal mechanisms.

analogies.analogy: Like different approaches to cooking - some follow strict recipes step-by-step (imperative), some describe the dish they want (declarative), some organize by ingredients and cooking methods (object-oriented).
Like a recipe card - a self-contained set of instructions that takes inputs (ingredients), processes them, and produces an output (dish). Can be reused for multiple meals.
Like a blueprinted house (class) and the actual house built from it (object). One blueprint can create many houses.
Like a secure vault - data is stored inside and can only be accessed through controlled methods (the vault's access mechanisms).
